info.title: Query API
info.description:
The Query API provides access to epilot's business analytics capabilities, enabling teams to query entity operations, workflow executions, and automation data stored in the epilot data lake.

**Key capabilities**

- **Entity queries** – Aggregate and filter entity operation events (create, update, delete) and point-in-time entity snapshots using `executeEntitiesQuery`.
- **Workflow analytics** – Query workflow execution counts, time series, phase durations, and task overviews using `executeWorkflowsQuery`.
- **Automation analytics** – Inspect automation execution history and list automation definitions via `executeAutomationQuery`.
- **Datalake V2 views** – Create and manage materialized SQL views over epilot data using `createOrUpdateView` and related endpoints.
- **BI tool integration** – Generate and manage ClickHouse credentials for connecting external BI tools (e.g. Tableau, Power BI) directly to the epilot data warehouse.
- **Semantic model** – Retrieve the semantic data model for agent/tool consumption via `getSemanticModel`.

**Concepts**

- **Measures** – Quantitative values to aggregate (e.g. `count_operations`, `count_entities`).
- **Dimensions** – Categorical groupings (e.g. `entity_attribute`, `time_with_granularity`).
- **Filters** – Constraints narrowing the dataset (e.g. time range, entity schema, workflow state).
- **Datasets** – Named data sources available for querying. Discover them with `listAvailableDatasetsV2`.

**Authentication**

All endpoints require a valid epilot Bearer token.

tags.name: Datasets
tags.description: Endpoints for discovering available datasets grouped by domain (entity, workflow, etc.)

tags.name: Query
tags.description:
Execute analytical queries against epilot entity and workflow datasets.

Use measures, dimensions, and filters to aggregate and slice data for dashboards, reports, and business intelligence.

tags.name: V1
tags.description: V1 query endpoints (maintained for backwards compatibility – prefer V2 endpoints for new integrations)

tags.name: V2
tags.description: V2 query endpoints including semantic model, autocomplete, and direct SQL-like query execution

tags.name: Datalake V2
tags.description:
Manage materialized SQL views and table relationships in the epilot data lake.

Views expose entity, taxonomy, and workflow data as queryable tables that can be used by BI tools connecting via ClickHouse credentials.

tags.name: CredentialsV2
tags.description: Manage ClickHouse credentials for connecting external BI tools directly to the epilot data warehouse

post.description:
Create or update a materialized SQL view in the epilot data lake.

Views expose epilot data (entities, taxonomies, workflow executions) as queryable tables that can be used by BI tools or queried directly. If a view with the same `view_name` already exists, it is updated.

Current Entities View.summary: Create a view for current entities

Workflow Executions View.summary: Create a view for workflow executions

get.description: Retrieve all materialized data lake views configured for the organization.
Retrieve the definition and attributes of a specific data lake view by its slug.

parameters.name: view_slug
parameters.description: The URL-safe slug identifier of the view

post.description:
Define or update a relationship between two tables in the data lake.

Relationships enable JOIN operations in views and queries. If a relationship with the same `relationship_name` already exists, it is updated.

get.description: Retrieve all table relationships configured for the organization's data lake.

Lists all available datasets grouped by domain

Current domains are:
 - Entity
 - Workflow

200.description: List of available datasets for the user

post.description:
Execute queries against entities datasets.

**Measures** are referred to as quantitative data, such as number of unique entities, operations, sum of profit, and so on.

**Dimensions** are referred to as categorical data, such as workflow status, product name, or units of time (e.g., day, week, month).

**Filters** are used to narrow down the dataset to be more specific e.g. only to a time range or specific entity schemas.

get.description: Retrieves the workflow phases associated with a given workflow definition ID.

parameters.name: workflowDefinitionId
parameters.description: The ID of the workflow definition to retrieve phases for.

200.description: A list of workflow phases associated with the specified definition ID.

id.description: The unique identifier of the workflow phase.

name.description: The name of the workflow phase.

get.description: Lists available worflow definitions with their ids, names and start times
Lists phase names of an org.

post.description:
Execute queries against datasets.

**Measures** are referred to as quantitative data, such as number of unique entities, operations, sum of profit, and so on.

**Dimensions** are referred to as categorical data, such as workflow status, product name, or units of time (e.g., day, week, month).

**Filters** are used to narrow down the dataset to be more specific e.g. only to a time range or specific entity schemas.
Generate credentials for the epilot datalake for connecting other BI tools with ClickHouse

get.description: List all the credentialof Clickhouse for the organization here

post.description: Revoke credentials for the epilot datalake for connecting other BI tools with Clickhouse

Execute queries against datasets.

**Measures** are referred to as quantitative data, such as number of unique entities, operations, sum of profit, and so on.

**Dimensions** are referred to as categorical data, such as workflow status, product name, or units of time (e.g., day, week, month).

**Filters** are used to narrow down the dataset to be more specific e.g. only to a time range or specific entity schemas.

get.description:
Get the semantic model for agent/tool consumption.

Returns available entities, their relationships, and supported query capabilities
including aggregations, calculation types, date granularities, and filter operators.

200.description: Semantic model with entities, relationships, and capabilities

get.description:
Generic autocomplete endpoint for querying distinct values across datasets.
Use the dataset parameter to specify which data category to query.

parameters.name: dataset
parameters.description: The dataset category to autocomplete against

parameters.name: attribute
parameters.description: Attribute to autocomplete in table.column format (e.g., current_purposes.name)

parameters.name: size
parameters.description: Maximum number of results to return

parameters.name: size
parameters.description: Maximum number of results to return

flow_ids.title: Automation Flow IDs
flow_ids.description: Filter by specific automation executions passing a list of automation flow ids.

execution_states.title: Automation Execution State
execution_states.description: Filter by the states of the automation executions. Use "scheduled" to filter executions that have scheduled actions. Multiple states are combined with OR logic.

search.description: Search term to filter automation definitions

WorkflowsQueryOptions.description: Settings for the data of the chart that is being queried.

workflow_definition_ids.title: Workflow Definition IDs
workflow_definition_ids.description: Filter by specific workflows by passing a list of workflow definition ids.

states.title: Workflow Definition IDs
states.description:
Filter by the states of the executions, phases, or tasks.
examples:
  - ["STARTED"]
  - ["COMPLETED", "PARTIALLY_COMPLETED"]

group_time_by.title: Group Time By
group_time_by.description: If chosen a time series, this parameter sets by which time the data is grouped, e.g. quarterly, monthly or weekly.  Available values: "D","W","M","Q","Y"

relative_time_range.title: Relative Time Range
relative_time_range.description: If chosen a time series, this arg defines the time span, e.g.  show data of the current month. Either this param or "time_range_from" and "time_range_to" must be set.

time_range_from.title: Time Range From
time_range_from.description: If chosen a time series, specify the start date in the format YYYY-MM-DD, e.g. 2023-01-01.

time_range_to.title: Time Range To
time_range_to.description: If chosen a time series, specify the end date in the format YYYY-MM-DD, e.g. 2023-03-31.

duration_threshold.title: Duration Threshold
duration_threshold.description: Certain datasets enable you to set a threshold of the duration. Example: We want to see total phase counts, phase counts less than threshold, phase counts more than threshold; threshold could be for example 45 days.

DatasetOptions.TotalCountOfWorkflowExecu.title: Dataset Options: Total Count of Workflow Executions
DatasetOptions.TotalCountOfWorkflowExecu.description: See the total of workflow executions in a pie chart.

DatasetOptions.WorkflowExecutionTimeSeri.title: Dataset Options: Workflow Execution Time Series

DatasetOptions.CumulativeSumOfWorkflows.title: Dataset Options: Cumulative Sum of Workflows

DatasetOptions.CancellationReasons.title: Dataset Options: Cancellation Reasons
DatasetOptions.CancellationReasons.description: See the cancellation reasons of workflows in a pie chart.

DatasetOptions.WorkflowTasksOverview.title: Dataset Options: Workflow Tasks Overview
DatasetOptions.WorkflowTasksOverview.description: Get a list of tasks to help organize executing users their day.

due_date_from.title: Due Date From
due_date_from.description: If chosen a time series, specify the start date in the format YYYY-MM-DD, e.g. 2023-01-01.

due_date_to.title: Due Date To
due_date_to.description: If chosen a time series, specify the end date in the format YYYY-MM-DD, e.g. 2023-03-31.

ECPDetails.description: Details regarding ECP for the workflow step

AutomationConfig.description: Configuration for automation execution to run

flowId.description: Id of the configured automation to run

executionId.description: Id of the automation execution which ran

executionStatus.description: Status of Automation Execution. Types can be found in Automation API

DynamicDueDate.description: set a Duedate for a step then a specific

TaskRequirement.description: describe the requirement for step enablement

assignedToInProgress.description: The user which moved the step/task to the IN_PROGRESS state. The user should also be present in the assignedTo property of the step/task

enabled.description: enabled flag results from calculating the requirements

WorkflowsQueryOptions.WorkflowDefinition.title: Workflow Definition IDs
WorkflowsQueryOptions.WorkflowDefinition.description: Filter by specific workflows by passing a list of workflow definition ids.

WorkflowsQueryOptions.EntitySchema.title: Entity Schema
WorkflowsQueryOptions.EntitySchema.description:
Workflows can be applied to different kinds of entities, e.g. on Opportunities or Orders.
You can choose to limit your results to workflow executions to specific entity schemas / types.

WorkflowsQueryOptions.RelativeTimeRange.title: Relative Time Range
WorkflowsQueryOptions.RelativeTimeRange.description: A combination of two worde that describe a time range, e.g. "this year" or "last month"

WorkflowsQueryOptions.TimeRangeFrom.title: Time Range From
WorkflowsQueryOptions.TimeRangeFrom.description: Set a specific start of a time range, e.g. "2023-01-01"

WorkflowsQueryOptions.TimeRangeTo.title: Time Range To
WorkflowsQueryOptions.TimeRangeTo.description: Set a specific end of a time range, e.g. "2023-06-30"

WorkflowsQueryOptions.GroupTimeBy.title: Group Time By
WorkflowsQueryOptions.GroupTimeBy.description: If chosen a time series, this parameter sets by which time the data is grouped, e.g. quarterly, monthly or weekly.  Available values: "D","W","M","Q","Y"

WorkflowsQueryOptions.WorkflowStates.title: Workflow States
WorkflowsQueryOptions.WorkflowStates.description: Filter by the states of the workflow executions.

updated_at.description: Timestamp when the execution was last updated

hits.description: Total number of automation definitions found

flow_id.description: Unique identifier for the automation flow

entity_schema.description: Entity schema associated with the flow

SemanticModel.description: The semantic model exposing entities, relationships, and query capabilities

entities.description: Available entity tables that can be queried

relationships.description: Relationships between entities for JOIN operations

BadRequestResponse.description: Bad request – invalid or missing parameters in the request payload

UnauthorizedResponse.description: Unauthorized – missing or invalid authentication credentials

ForbiddenResponse.description: Forbidden – caller does not have permission to perform this action

example.message: Insufficient permissions to access this resource
